# AetherEdge

> Build the deterministic edge foundation for agents to turn human intent into
> governed, verifiable physical behavior.
> Multi-protocol device acquisition, a sub-millisecond shared-memory data
> plane, isolated runtime processes, and an MCP server let AI agents query and
> (with explicit authorization) operate the system. Energy management is an
> optional domain pack, not a core dependency.

AetherEdge is the edge product in the AetherIoT platform. It is headless and publishes machine-readable runtime and application
contracts, an Agent Skill, MCP tools/resources, and online Markdown so AI
agents can build fit-for-purpose clients without turning a Web UI into a
second authority. AetherEMS is the independent official energy distribution.
The complete end-user conversational agent, intent compiler, simulation, and
continuous-adaptation loop remain product direction rather than shipped beta
capabilities.

For Data Processing, `as_of` is an event-time frame bound. The current SQLite
history has no ingestion/source epoch and artifact provenance has no
training/availability cut, so historical backtests require frozen history and
artifact sets; do not infer point-in-time safety from `as_of` or a digest.
Persisted historian storage settings are saved intent and require
reconnect/restart plus sentinel verification before they represent the active
writer. Production direct-SQLite access additionally requires a dedicated
read-only historian directory/identity for `aether-api`; the base
`/app/data:rw` Compose mount is not that boundary.

## AI-native Architecture

- [Canonical Agent Instructions](AGENTS.md): Architecture boundaries, safety rules, Rust conventions, and verification commands
- [AetherIoT Platform Overview](docs/overview/platform.md): Product family, authority boundaries, and naming rules
- [AI-native Platform](docs/overview/ai-native-platform.md): Human intent, governed generation, deterministic execution, and the boundary between current foundations and planned end-user experience
- [AetherEdge Agent Skill](skills/aether-iot/SKILL.md): Contract-first workflow for onboarding, SDK integrations, generated applications, and governed commands
- [AetherIoT to AetherEdge Migration](docs/migration/aetheriot-to-aetheredge.md): Repository rename, stable identifiers, rollout, and rollback
- [Product Compatibility](docs/compatibility/version-matrix.md): Tested Edge, Contracts, and Cloud combinations
- [Platform Status](docs/roadmap/status.md): Implemented, experimental, and planned capabilities across products
- [Architecture Entry Point](ARCHITECTURE.md): Target runtime and dependency direction
- [ADR-0001](docs/adr/0001-ai-native-edge-kernel.md): Decision to migrate to an industry-neutral, external-database-free edge kernel
- [ADR-0002](docs/adr/0002-dataplane-and-local-outbox.md): Physical SHM extraction, local journal durability, and MQTT delivery boundary
- [ADR-0004](docs/adr/0004-canonical-service-names.md): Breaking canonical identities for the six isolated runtime services
- [ADR-0006](docs/adr/0006-installation-and-commissioning.md): Fail-safe installation context, atomic configuration apply, and the boundary between setup and physical-device commissioning
- [ADR-0007](docs/adr/0007-aether-core-and-ems-distribution.md): Staged separation of the Aether kernel and AetherEMS energy distribution without rewriting Git history
- [ADR-0009](docs/adr/0009-aether-data-processing.md): Accepted industry-neutral task, processor direction, derived-data authority, opt-in API/sidecar composition, and reserved future runtime decision
- [ADR-0012](docs/adr/0012-agent-first-application-surface.md): Headless Kernel, Agent Skill, generated-client, and single remote application boundary
- [ADR-0013](docs/adr/0013-single-sdk-source-release.md): Single SDK facade, source-only workspace packages, and signed Git/source distribution
- [ADR-0014](docs/adr/0014-coordinated-shm-topology-publication.md): Coordinated point/health SHM publication epochs and rolling compatibility
- [ADR-0015](docs/adr/0015-configuration-authority-and-reconciliation.md): Configuration authority, revisioned mutation, and applied-state reconciliation
- [ADR-0017](docs/adr/0017-experimental-cloudlink-mqtt-edge-foundation.md): Experimental broker-neutral CloudLink MQTT edge foundation, application ACK spool, topic ACLs, migration, and remaining release gates
- [ADR-0018](docs/adr/0018-pinned-aethercontracts-consumption.md): Digest-pinned public contract authority, offline consumption, partial-import evidence, and legacy/no-control safeguards
- [Target Repository Layout](docs/architecture/target-layout.md): Core crates, optional extensions, interfaces, and domain packs
- [Pack Contract Crate](crates/aether-pack/README.md): Versioned manifest loading, release compatibility, required identifiers, fail-safe examples, and pack-root path confinement
- [SHM Data Plane Crate](crates/aether-dataplane/README.md): Layout ownership, mmap validation, seqlock safety, and compatibility imports
- [Data Processing Codec Crate](crates/aether-data-processing/README.md): Strict transport-neutral v1 JSON codec, DTO conversion, and RFC 8785 input digests
- [CloudLink Candidate Codec](crates/aether-cloudlink/README.md): Experimental transport-neutral sessions, delivery envelopes, canonical business digests, Runtime Manifest, and PointSample mapping
- [CloudLink MQTT Binding](extensions/cloudlink-mqtt/README.md): User-selected MQTT v3.1.1 broker, TLS/auth, exact topics, QoS 1 PUBACK evidence, reconnect, and opt-in integration harness
- [CloudLink Candidate Contracts](contracts/cloudlink/README.md): Product integration copies for the digest-pinned public contract subset plus non-authoritative migration proposals
- [Local Store Extension](extensions/store-local/README.md): Memory adapters and the crash-recoverable FileOutbox
- [SQLite History Query Extension](extensions/sqlite-history-query/README.md): Default lazy read-only history adapter with task-scoped interval aggregation in one invocation-time SQLite snapshot, plus explicit bitemporal and active-writer limitations
- [HTTP History Query Extension](extensions/http-history-query/README.md): Optional loopback adapter for an upstream pre-aligned Last/Reject cadence grid
- [HTTP Data Processor Extension](extensions/http-data-processor/README.md): Optional bounded local/remote HTTP implementation of the DataProcessor port
- [Data Processing Contract Schemas](contracts/data-processing/README.md): Strict Draft 2020-12 schemas for caller requests, complete frames, processor requests/results, derived data, and errors
- [AetherEMS Energy Data Processing Assets](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/data-processing/README.md): Energy-owned load/PV task declarations, bindings, and conformance fixtures
- [AI Component Catalog](ai/catalog.yaml): Machine-readable component ownership, paths, and narrow verification commands
- [AI Invariants](ai/invariants.md): Source-of-truth, control safety, offline, and dependency invariants
- [Capability Safety Policy](ai/safety-policy.yaml): Machine-readable risk and authorization policy
- [Data Processing AI Eval Scenarios](ai/evals/data-processing.yaml): Permission, confirmation, audit, non-idempotency, untrusted-result, isolation, and no-control cases tied to deterministic Rust tests
- [Add a Data Processor](ai/runbooks/add-data-processor.md): Deterministic procedure for typed tasks, processor isolation, conformance, AI metadata, and verification
- [OceanClaw + nex-env IoT Safety Lab](docs/plans/2026-07-11-oceanclaw-nex-env-iot-safety-lab.md): Backlog proposal for using Aether as an isolated IoT agent-safety experiment and device-state oracle

## Domain Knowledge

- [AetherEMS Energy Storage Primer](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/knowledge/ess-primer.md): How ESS concepts - PCS, BMS, SOC, grid interface - map onto Aether products, instances, and points
- [AetherEMS Product Models](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/knowledge/product-models.md): Energy Pack-owned product models and point semantics
- [AetherEMS Control Strategies](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/knowledge/control-strategies.md): Expressing SOC management, peak shaving, and demand control as executable rule flows
- [Safe Operations for AI Agents](docs/guides/safe-operations.md): Generic query, command, permission, confirmation, idempotency, and audit rules
- [AetherEMS Safe Operations](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/knowledge/safe-operations.md): Energy-specific constraints for commands that can reach physical equipment
- [AetherEMS Power Forecasting](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/knowledge/power-forecasting.md): Map load and PV data into the request-driven Load-Forecasting processor without creating a second data plane

## Concepts

- [System Architecture](docs/concepts/architecture.md): Six isolated edge services, their ports, and their SHM, UDS, SQLite, HTTP, and MQTT paths
- [Data Model](docs/concepts/data-model.md): Products, instances, and T/S/C/A points - and why an instance is a pure thing-model with no status field
- [Shared Memory](docs/concepts/shared-memory.md): The SHM data plane - slot layout, writer ownership, seqlock reads, generations, and the PointWatch event plane
- [Rule Engine](docs/concepts/rule-engine.md): Dual-column rule storage, tick and event scheduling, execution, and hot reload
- [Data Flow](docs/concepts/data-flow.md): SHM-native uplink and downlink paths end to end, with latency budgets and optional mirror boundaries
- [Data Processing](docs/concepts/data-processing.md): The implemented cross-industry data-processing capability, its typed task boundary, data authority, and non-goals
- [Data Processing Flow](docs/concepts/data-processing-flow.md): Interval-end data assembly, processor requests, result validation, direct v1 response, and control separation end to end

## Guides

- [Getting Started](docs/guides/getting-started.md): Build the workspace, initialize configuration, start services, and verify health
- [Build Applications with AI](docs/guides/build-applications-with-ai.md): Generate contract-first clients without creating a second state or safety authority
- [Connect Devices](docs/guides/connect-devices.md): Configure channels, choose protocols, and map device points to instances
- [Writing Rules](docs/guides/writing-rules.md): Author rules through the HTTP API or the visual flow editor
- [Connect AI Assistants](docs/guides/ai-assistants.md): Point Claude or any MCP client at aether mcp, and choose between read-only and write access
- [Deployment](docs/guides/deployment.md): Run with Docker Compose or build a self-contained installer for edge devices
- [Data Processors](docs/guides/data-processors.md): Declare data-processing tasks in domain packs and connect request-driven local or remote processors
- [AetherEMS Data Processing Runtime Template](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/packs/energy/data-processing/runtime.example.yaml): Energy-owned composition template for SQLite history, snapshot covariates, audit, and the Load-Forecasting HTTP processor
- [AetherEMS Load-Forecasting Processor](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/processors/load-forecasting/README.md): Request-driven forecasting implementation without reverse data reads
- [AetherEMS Load-Forecasting Deployment](https://github.com/EvanL1/AetherEMS/blob/main/processors/load-forecasting/deploy/README.md): Commission the digest-pinned, authenticated processor with the production Compose override or an isolated systemd unit

## Reference

- [CLI Reference](docs/reference/cli.md): Every aether command - services, sync, doctor, channels, rules, and more
- [MCP Tools Reference](docs/reference/mcp-tools.md): All MCP tools grouped by domain, with parameters and write-safety markers
- [Configuration Reference](docs/reference/configuration.md): YAML configuration schema, the sync pipeline, and environment variables
- [HTTP API](docs/reference/http-api.md): Response envelopes, authentication, and the opt-in `/api/v1/data-processing/*` application routes
- [Data Processing Contracts](docs/reference/data-processing-contracts.md): Implemented v1 contracts for processing frames, requests, derived results, validation, and failure semantics
- [CloudLink MQTT v1 Reference](docs/reference/cloudlink-mqtt-v1.md): Complete alpha.3 fixtures, real dual-harness fault evidence, spool semantics, telemetry mapping, and unresolved production authentication/durability
- [Data Processing Implementation Record](docs/plans/2026-07-11-aether-data-processing.md): Documentation-first implementation sequence, landed baseline, and remaining production commissioning/shadow-cutover gates
